entities.name: Baell
entities.description: A king of the power of the east who appears with three heads (toad, man, and cat) and commands 66 legions.

entities.name: Agares
entities.description: A duke of the power of the east appearing as a fair old man riding a crocodile.

entities.name: Marbas
entities.description: A great president appearing as a mighty lion or a man.

entities.name: Amon
entities.description: A mighty marques appearing as a wolf with a serpent's tail.

entities.name: Barbatos
entities.description: A duke or earl who understands the voices of animals.

entities.name: Buer
entities.description: A president who teaches philosophy and the virtue of herbs.

entities.name: Paimon
entities.description: A king who is more obedient to Lucifer than other kings.

power: knows the disposition of the world

entities.name: Beliall
entities.description: A king created immediately after Lucifer.

entities.name: Salomon
entities.description: A wise king who gathered demons in a brass vessel.

achievements: gathered devils in a brass vessel
used divine power to constrain spirits

entities.name: conjuration
entities.description: The art of calling up spirits, angels, or devils.

entities.name: Alchemists
entities.description: A group criticized for their vain hopes and the destruction of wealth through their art.

criticism: dream and dote after double advantage
their art turns metals into mist and smoke

relationships.relation: is the father and seducer of

summary: This passage from Reginald Scot's 'The Discoverie of Witchcraft' provides a detailed hierarchy of demonic entities, including their ranks, appearances, and specific powers. It describes the art of conjuration as a method to summon spirits and details various figures like Baell, Paimon, and Beliall, while also critiquing alchemists and discussing the legendary exploits of King Solomon.
key_passages: These are no small fooles, they go not to worke with a baggage tode, or a cat, as Wierusin witches doo; but with a kind of majestic, and with authoritie they do call up by name, and have at their commandement seventie and nine principall and princelie divels.
He [Paimon] is constrained by divine vertue to stand before the exorcist; where he putteth on the likenesse of a man.
